rawMembers.name: interface/touchscreen/enable_long_press_as_right_click
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], long press on touchscreen is treated as right click. [b]Note:[/b] Defaults to [code]true[/code] on touchscreen devices.

rawMembers.name: interface/touchscreen/enable_pan_and_scale_gestures
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], enable two finger pan and scale gestures on touchscreen devices. [b]Note:[/b] Defaults to [code]true[/code] on touchscreen devices.

rawMembers.name: interface/touchscreen/enable_touch_optimizations
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], increases the scrollbar touch area, enables a larger dragger for split containers, and increases PopupMenu vertical separation to improve usability on touchscreen devices. [b]Note:[/b] Defaults to [code]true[/code] on touchscreen devices.

rawMembers.name: interface/touchscreen/haptic_on_long_press
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], the device will vibrate when a long-press gesture triggers a right-click context menu in the editor. [b]Note:[/b] Only has an effect on devices with haptic feedback hardware. Defaults to [code]true[/code] on touchscreen devices.

rawMembers.name: interface/touchscreen/scale_gizmo_handles
rawMembers.summary: Specify the multiplier to apply to the scale for the editor gizmo handles to improve usability on touchscreen devices. [b]Note:[/b] Defaults to [code]1[/code] on non-touchscreen devices.

rawMembers.name: interface/touchscreen/touch_actions_panel
rawMembers.summary: A touch-friendly panel that provides easy access to common actions such as save, delete, undo, and redo without requiring a keyboard. [b]Note:[/b] Only available in the Android and XR editor.

rawMembers.name: network/connection/check_for_updates
rawMembers.summary: Specifies how the engine should check for updates. - [b]Disable Update Checks[/b] will block the engine from checking updates (see also [member network/connection/network_mode]). - [b]Auto[/b] (default) will check for newest stable or unstable version, depending on which version are you currently using. Switch to another option if you want to lock in. - [b]Check Newest Preview[/b] will check for the newest available development snapshot. - [b]Check Newest Stable[/b] will check for the newest available stable version. - [b]Check Newest Patch[/b] will check for the latest available stable version, but only within the same minor version. E.g. if your version is [code]4.3.stable[/code], you will be notified about [code]4.3.1.stable[/code], but not [code]4.4.stable[/code]. All update modes will ignore builds with different major versions (e.g. Godot 4 -> Godot 5).

rawMembers.name: network/connection/network_mode
rawMembers.summary: Determines whether online features, such as the Asset Store or update checks, are enabled in the editor. If this is a privacy concern, disabling these online features prevents the editor from making HTTP requests to the Godot website or third-party platforms hosting assets from the Asset Store. Editor plugins and tool scripts are recommended to follow this setting. However, Godot can't prevent them from violating this rule.

rawMembers.name: network/debug/remote_host
rawMembers.summary: The address to listen to when starting the remote debugger. This can be set to this device's local IP address to allow external clients to connect to the remote debugger (instead of restricting the remote debugger to connections from [code]localhost[/code]).

rawMembers.name: network/debug/remote_port
rawMembers.summary: The port to listen to when starting the remote debugger. Godot will try to use port numbers above the configured number if the configured number is already taken by another application.

rawMembers.name: network/http_proxy/host
rawMembers.summary: The host to use to contact the HTTP and HTTPS proxy in the editor (for the asset store and export template downloads). See also [member network/http_proxy/port]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot currently doesn't automatically use system proxy settings, so you have to enter them manually here if needed.

rawMembers.name: network/http_proxy/port
rawMembers.summary: The port number to use to contact the HTTP and HTTPS proxy in the editor (for the asset store and export template downloads). See also [member network/http_proxy/host]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot currently doesn't automatically use system proxy settings, so you have to enter them manually here if needed.

rawMembers.name: network/language_server/enable_smart_resolve
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code] the language server will try to provide additional results when resolving symbols at the cost of showing wrong results. All symbols in the project are checked and resolved just based on their name, without taking context into account. [codeblock] func untyped(param): param.print() # Will resolve to the global print method for e.g. hover hints. [/codeblock] When using static typing it is recommended to disable this setting, since it will mostly add false positives for typed code. [b]Note:[/b] This setting also influences how symbols are resolved when using renaming capabilities. [b]Note:[/b] The default value of this setting might change in future versions.

rawMembers.name: network/language_server/poll_limit_usec
rawMembers.summary: The upper limit of time, that the language server spends for IO each poll.

rawMembers.name: network/language_server/remote_host
rawMembers.summary: The host used to listen for language server clients.

rawMembers.name: network/language_server/remote_port
rawMembers.summary: The port used to listen for language server clients. [b]Note:[/b] A port configured with command-line options will take priority over this setting: [code]--lsp-port <port>[/code].

rawMembers.name: network/language_server/show_native_symbols_in_editor
rawMembers.summary: The declaration of native symbols can't be resolved to a position in the file system. If [code]true[/code] the language server will instead open the documentation for native symbols in the editor. [b]Note:[/b] The VSCode plugin adds additional functionality which allows viewing Godot documentation directly in VSCode, so this option is usually not needed in VSCode.

rawMembers.name: network/language_server/use_thread
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code] the language server will run in a separate thread, if [code]false[/code] it will run on the main thread.

rawMembers.name: network/tls/editor_tls_certificates
rawMembers.summary: The TLS certificate bundle to use for HTTP requests made within the editor (e.g. from the Asset Store tab). If left empty, the [url=https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/thirdparty/certs/ca-bundle.crt]included Mozilla certificate bundle[/url] will be used.

rawMembers.name: network/tls/enable_tls_v1.3
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], enable TLSv1.3 negotiation. [b]Note:[/b] Only supported when using Mbed TLS 3.0 or later (Linux distribution packages may be compiled against older system Mbed TLS packages), otherwise the maximum supported TLS version is always TLSv1.2.

rawMembers.name: project_manager/default_renderer
rawMembers.summary: The renderer type that will be checked off by default when creating a new project. Accepted strings are "forward_plus", "mobile" or "gl_compatibility".

rawMembers.name: project_manager/directory_naming_convention
rawMembers.summary: Directory naming convention for the project manager. Options are "No Convention" (project name is directory name), "kebab-case" (default), "snake_case", "camelCase", "PascalCase", or "Title Case".

rawMembers.name: project_manager/sorting_order
rawMembers.summary: The sorting order to use in the project manager. When changing the sorting order in the project manager, this setting is set permanently in the editor settings.

rawMembers.name: run/auto_save/save_before_running
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], saves all scenes and scripts automatically before running the project. Setting this to [code]false[/code] prevents the editor from saving if there are no changes which can speed up the project startup slightly, but it makes it possible to run a project that has unsaved changes. (Unsaved changes will not be visible in the running project.)

rawMembers.name: run/bottom_panel/action_on_play
rawMembers.summary: The action to execute on the bottom panel when running the project. [b]Note:[/b] This option won't do anything if the bottom panel switching is locked using the pin button in the corner of the bottom panel.

rawMembers.name: run/bottom_panel/action_on_stop
rawMembers.summary: The action to execute on the bottom panel when stopping the project. [b]Note:[/b] This option won't do anything if the bottom panel switching is locked using the pin button in the corner of the bottom panel.

rawMembers.name: run/output/always_clear_output_on_play
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], the editor will clear the Output panel when running the project.

rawMembers.name: run/output/font_size
rawMembers.summary: The size of the font in the [b]Output[/b] panel at the bottom of the editor. This setting does not impact the font size of the script editor (see [member interface/editor/fonts/code_font_size]).

rawMembers.name: run/output/max_lines
rawMembers.summary: Maximum number of lines to show at any one time in the Output panel.

rawMembers.name: run/platforms/linuxbsd/prefer_wayland
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], on Linux/BSD, the editor will check for Wayland first instead of X11 (if available).

rawMembers.name: run/window_placement/android_window
rawMembers.summary: Specifies how the Play window is launched relative to the Android editor. - [b]Auto (based on screen size)[/b] (default) will automatically choose how to launch the Play window based on the device and screen metrics. Defaults to [b]Same as Editor[/b] on phones and [b]Side-by-side with Editor[/b] on tablets. - [b]Same as Editor[/b] will launch the Play window in the same window as the Editor. - [b]Side-by-side with Editor[/b] will launch the Play window side-by-side with the Editor window. [b]Note:[/b] Only available in the Android editor.

rawMembers.name: run/window_placement/game_embed_mode
rawMembers.summary: Overrides game embedding setting for all newly opened projects. If enabled, game embedding settings are not saved.

rawMembers.name: run/window_placement/rect
rawMembers.summary: The window mode to use to display the project when starting the project from the editor. [b]Note:[/b] Game embedding is not available for [b]"Force Maximized"[/b] or [b]"Force Fullscreen"[/b].

rawMembers.name: run/window_placement/rect_custom_position
rawMembers.summary: The custom position to use when starting the project from the editor (in pixels from the top-left corner). Only effective if [member run/window_placement/rect] is set to [b]Custom Position[/b].

rawMembers.name: run/window_placement/screen
rawMembers.summary: The monitor to display the project on when starting the project from the editor.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/caret/caret_blink
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], makes the caret blink according to [member text_editor/appearance/caret/caret_blink_interval]. Disabling this setting can improve battery life on laptops if you spend long amounts of time in the script editor, since it will reduce the frequency at which the editor needs to be redrawn.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/caret/caret_blink_interval
rawMembers.summary: The interval at which the caret will blink (in seconds). See also [member text_editor/appearance/caret/caret_blink].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/caret/highlight_all_occurrences
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], highlights all occurrences of the currently selected text in the script editor. See also [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/word_highlighted_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/caret/highlight_current_line
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], colors the background of the line the caret is currently on with [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/current_line_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/caret/type
rawMembers.summary: The shape of the caret to use in the script editor. [b]Line[/b] displays a vertical line to the left of the current character, whereas [b]Block[/b] displays an outline over the current character.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/drag_and_drop_info/show_drag_and_drop_info
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], shows an info label listing available drop options when dragging an object into the script text editor.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/enable_inline_color_picker
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays a colored button before any [Color] constructor in the script editor. Clicking on them allows the color to be modified through a color picker.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/guidelines/line_length_guideline_hard_column
rawMembers.summary: The column at which to display a subtle line as a line length guideline for scripts. This should generally be greater than [member text_editor/appearance/guidelines/line_length_guideline_soft_column].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/guidelines/line_length_guideline_soft_column
rawMembers.summary: The column at which to display a [i]very[/i] subtle line as a line length guideline for scripts. This should generally be lower than [member text_editor/appearance/guidelines/line_length_guideline_hard_column].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/guidelines/show_line_length_guidelines
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays line length guidelines to help you keep line lengths in check. See also [member text_editor/appearance/guidelines/line_length_guideline_soft_column] and [member text_editor/appearance/guidelines/line_length_guideline_hard_column].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/gutters/highlight_type_safe_lines
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], highlights type-safe lines by displaying their line number color with [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/safe_line_number_color] instead of [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/line_number_color]. Type-safe lines are lines of code where the type of all variables is known at compile-time. These type-safe lines may run faster thanks to typed instructions.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/gutters/line_numbers_zero_padded
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays line numbers with zero padding (e.g. [code]007[/code] instead of [code]7[/code]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/gutters/show_info_gutter
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays a gutter at the left containing icons for methods with signal connections and for overridden methods.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/gutters/show_line_numbers
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays line numbers in a gutter at the left.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/lines/autowrap_mode
rawMembers.summary: If [member text_editor/appearance/lines/word_wrap] is set to [code]1[/code], sets text wrapping mode. To see how each mode behaves, see [enum TextServer.AutowrapMode].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/lines/code_folding
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays the folding arrows next to indented code sections and allows code folding. If [code]false[/code], hides the folding arrows next to indented code sections and disallows code folding.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/lines/word_wrap
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], wraps long lines over multiple lines to avoid horizontal scrolling. This is a display-only feature; it does not actually insert line breaks in your scripts.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/minimap/minimap_width
rawMembers.summary: The width of the minimap in the script editor (in pixels).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/minimap/show_minimap
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], draws an overview of the script near the scroll bar. The minimap can be left-clicked to scroll directly to a location in an "absolute" manner.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/whitespace/draw_spaces
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], draws space characters as centered points.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/whitespace/draw_tabs
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], draws tab characters as chevrons.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/appearance/whitespace/line_spacing
rawMembers.summary: The space to add between lines (in pixels). Greater line spacing can help improve readability at the cost of displaying fewer lines on screen. Negative values allow for even more compact text, but may look broken with certain fonts.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/documentation/enable_tooltips
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], documentation tooltips will appear when hovering over a symbol.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/auto_reload_and_parse_scripts_on_save
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], tool scripts will be automatically soft-reloaded after they are saved.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/auto_reload_scripts_on_external_change
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], automatically reloads scripts and text-based shaders in the editor when they have been modified and saved by external editors or tools and the editor regains focus. External changes can be discarded by using the Undo function after they've been loaded in the editor. If [code]false[/code], a file conflict dialog will always be displayed when the editor regains focus. This dialog allows you to choose whether to keep local changes or discard them. [b]Note:[/b] Even when this setting is [code]true[/code], a file conflict dialog is still displayed in certain situations. For instance, it will display when the script editor has unsaved changes that the external editor did not account for.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/autosave_interval_secs
rawMembers.summary: If set to a value greater than [code]0[/code], automatically saves the current script following the specified interval (in seconds). This can be used to prevent data loss if the editor crashes.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/convert_indent_on_save
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], converts indentation to match the script editor's indentation settings when saving a script. See also [member text_editor/behavior/indent/type].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/drop_preload_resources_as_uid
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], when dropping a [Resource] file to script editor while [kbd]Ctrl[/kbd] is held, the resource will be preloaded with a UID. If [code]false[/code], the resource will be preloaded with a path. When you hold [kbd]Ctrl+Shift[/kbd], the behavior is reversed.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/open_dominant_script_on_scene_change
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], opening a scene automatically opens the script attached to the root node, or the topmost node if the root has no script.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/restore_scripts_on_load
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], reopens scripts that were opened in the last session when the editor is reopened on a given project.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/trim_final_newlines_on_save
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], trims all empty newlines after the final newline when saving a script. Final newlines refer to the empty newlines found at the end of files. Since these serve no practical purpose, they can and should be removed to make version control diffs less noisy.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/files/trim_trailing_whitespace_on_save
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], trims trailing whitespace when saving a script. Trailing whitespace refers to tab and space characters placed at the end of lines. Since these serve no practical purpose, they can and should be removed to make version control diffs less noisy.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/general/empty_selection_clipboard
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], copying or cutting without a selection is performed on all lines with a caret. Otherwise, copy and cut require a selection.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/indent/auto_indent
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], automatically indents code when pressing the [kbd]Enter[/kbd] key based on blocks above the new line.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/indent/indent_wrapped_lines
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], all wrapped lines are indented to the same amount as the unwrapped line.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/indent/size
rawMembers.summary: When using tab indentation, determines the length of each tab. When using space indentation, determines how many spaces are inserted when pressing [kbd]Tab[/kbd] and when automatic indentation is performed.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/indent/type
rawMembers.summary: The indentation style to use (tabs or spaces). [b]Note:[/b] The [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_styleguide.html]GDScript style guide[/url] recommends using tabs for indentation. It is advised to change this setting only if you need to work on a project that currently uses spaces for indentation.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/custom_word_separators
rawMembers.summary: The characters to consider as word delimiters if [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/use_custom_word_separators] is [code]true[/code]. This is in addition to default characters if [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/use_default_word_separators] is [code]true[/code]. The characters should be defined without separation, for example [code]_♥=[/code].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/drag_and_drop_selection
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], allows drag-and-dropping text in the script editor to move text. Disable this if you find yourself accidentally drag-and-dropping text in the script editor.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/move_caret_on_right_click
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], the caret will be moved when right-clicking somewhere in the script editor (like when left-clicking or middle-clicking). If [code]false[/code], the caret will only be moved when left-clicking or middle-clicking somewhere.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/open_script_when_connecting_signal_to_existing_method
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], opens the script editor when connecting a signal to an existing script method from the Signals dock.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/scroll_past_end_of_file
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], allows scrolling past the end of the file.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/smooth_scrolling
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables a smooth scrolling animation when using the mouse wheel to scroll. See [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/v_scroll_speed] for the speed of this animation. [b]Note:[/b] [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/smooth_scrolling] currently behaves poorly in projects where [member ProjectSettings.physics/common/physics_ticks_per_second] has been increased significantly from its default value ([code]60[/code]). In this case, it is recommended to disable this setting.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/stay_in_script_editor_on_node_selected
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], prevents automatically switching between the Script and 2D/3D screens when selecting a node in the Scene tree dock.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/use_custom_word_separators
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses the characters in [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/custom_word_separators] as word separators for word navigation and operations. This is in addition to the default characters if [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/use_default_word_separators] is also enabled. Word navigation and operations include double-clicking on a word or holding [kbd]Ctrl[/kbd] ([kbd]Cmd[/kbd] on macOS) while pressing [kbd]left[/kbd], [kbd]right[/kbd], [kbd]backspace[/kbd], or [kbd]delete[/kbd].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/use_default_word_separators
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses the characters in [code]`!"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^`{|}~[/code], the Unicode General Punctuation table, and the Unicode CJK Punctuation table as word separators for word navigation and operations. If [code]false[/code], a subset of these characters are used and does not include the characters [code]<>$~^=+|[/code]. This is in addition to custom characters if [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/use_custom_word_separators] is also enabled. These characters are used to determine where a word stops. Word navigation and operations include double-clicking on a word or holding [kbd]Ctrl[/kbd] ([kbd]Cmd[/kbd] on macOS) while pressing [kbd]left[/kbd], [kbd]right[/kbd], [kbd]backspace[/kbd], or [kbd]delete[/kbd].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/behavior/navigation/v_scroll_speed
rawMembers.summary: The speed of scrolling in lines per second when [member text_editor/behavior/navigation/smooth_scrolling] is [code]true[/code]. Higher values make the script scroll by faster when using the mouse wheel. [b]Note:[/b] You can hold down [kbd]Alt[/kbd] while using the mouse wheel to temporarily scroll 5 times faster.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/add_node_path_literals
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses [NodePath] instead of [String] when appropriate for code autocompletion or for drag and dropping object properties into the script editor.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/add_string_name_literals
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses [StringName] instead of [String] when appropriate for code autocompletion.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/add_type_hints
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], automatically adds [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/static_typing.html]GDScript static typing[/url] (such as [code]-> void[/code] and [code]: int[/code]) in many situations where it's possible to, including when: - Accepting a suggestion from code autocompletion; - Creating a new script from a template; - Connecting signals from the Signals dock; - Creating variables prefixed with [annotation @GDScript.@onready], by dropping nodes from the Scene dock into the script editor while holding [kbd]Ctrl[/kbd].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/auto_brace_complete
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], automatically inserts the matching closing brace when the opening brace is inserted by typing or autocompletion. Also automatically removes the closing brace when pressing [kbd]Backspace[/kbd] on the opening brace. This includes brackets ([code]()[/code], [code][][/code], [code]{}[/code]), string quotation marks ([code]''[/code], [code]""[/code]), and comments ([code]/**/[/code]) if the language supports it.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/code_complete_delay
rawMembers.summary: The delay in seconds after which autocompletion suggestions should be displayed when the user stops typing.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/code_complete_enabled
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], code completion will be triggered automatically after [member text_editor/completion/code_complete_delay]. Even if [code]false[/code], code completion can be triggered manually with the [code]ui_text_completion_query[/code] action (by default [kbd]Ctrl + Space[/kbd] or [kbd]Cmd + Space[/kbd] on macOS).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/colorize_suggestions
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code] enables the coloring for some items in the autocompletion suggestions, like vector components.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/complete_file_paths
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], provides autocompletion suggestions for file paths in methods such as [code]load()[/code] and [code]preload()[/code].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/idle_parse_delay
rawMembers.summary: The delay in seconds after which the script editor should check for errors when the user stops typing.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/idle_parse_delay_with_errors_found
rawMembers.summary: The delay used instead of [member text_editor/completion/idle_parse_delay], when the parser has found errors. A lower value should feel more responsive while fixing code, but may cause notable stuttering and increase CPU usage.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/put_callhint_tooltip_below_current_line
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], the code completion tooltip will appear below the current line unless there is no space on screen below the current line. If [code]false[/code], the code completion tooltip will appear above the current line.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/completion/use_single_quotes
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], performs string autocompletion with single quotes. If [code]false[/code], performs string autocompletion with double quotes (which matches the [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_styleguide.html]GDScript style guide[/url]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/external/exec_flags
rawMembers.summary: The command-line arguments to pass to the external text editor that is run when [member text_editor/external/use_external_editor] is [code]true[/code]. See also [member text_editor/external/exec_path].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/external/exec_path
rawMembers.summary: The path to the text editor executable used to edit text files if [member text_editor/external/use_external_editor] is [code]true[/code].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/external/use_external_editor
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses an external editor instead of the built-in Script Editor. See also [member text_editor/external/exec_path] and [member text_editor/external/exec_flags].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/help/class_reference_examples
rawMembers.summary: Controls which multi-line code blocks should be displayed in the editor help. This setting does not affect single-line code literals in the editor help.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/help/help_font_size
rawMembers.summary: The font size to use for the editor help (built-in class reference).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/help/help_source_font_size
rawMembers.summary: The font size to use for code samples in the editor help (built-in class reference).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/help/help_title_font_size
rawMembers.summary: The font size to use for headings in the editor help (built-in class reference).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/help/show_help_index
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays a table of contents at the left of the editor help (at the location where the members overview would appear when editing a script).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/help/sort_functions_alphabetically
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], the script's method list in the Script Editor is sorted alphabetically.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/group_help_pages
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], class reference pages are grouped together at the bottom of the Script Editor's script list.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/highlight_scene_scripts
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], the scripts that are used by the current scene are highlighted in the Script Editor's script list.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/list_script_names_as
rawMembers.summary: Specifies how script paths should be displayed in Script Editor's script list. If using the "Name" option and some scripts share the same file name, more parts of their paths are revealed to avoid conflicts.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/script_temperature_enabled
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], the names of recently opened scripts in the Script Editor are highlighted with the accent color, with its intensity based on how recently they were opened.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/script_temperature_history_size
rawMembers.summary: How many script names can be highlighted at most, if [member text_editor/script_list/script_temperature_enabled] is [code]true[/code]. Scripts older than this value use the default font color.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/show_members_overview
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], displays an overview of the current script's member functions at the left of the script editor. See also [member text_editor/script_list/sort_members_outline_alphabetically].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/sort_members_outline_alphabetically
rawMembers.summary: If [code]true[/code], sorts the members outline (located at the left of the script editor) using alphabetical order. If [code]false[/code], sorts the members outline depending on the order in which members are found in the script. [b]Note:[/b] Only effective if [member text_editor/script_list/show_members_overview] is [code]true[/code].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/script_list/sort_scripts_by
rawMembers.summary: Specifies sorting used for Script Editor's open script list.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/color_theme
rawMembers.summary: The syntax theme to use in the script editor. You can save your own syntax theme from your current settings by using [b]File > Theme > Save As...[/b] at the top of the script editor. The syntax theme will then be available locally in the list of color themes. You can find additional syntax themes to install in the [url=https://github.com/godotengine/godot-syntax-themes]godot-syntax-themes[/url] repository.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/background_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background color. If set to a translucent color, the editor theme's base color will be visible behind.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/base_type_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's base type color (used for types like [Vector2], [Vector3], [Color], ...).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/bookmark_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's bookmark icon color (displayed in the gutter).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/brace_mismatch_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's brace mismatch color. Used when the caret is currently on a mismatched brace, parenthesis or bracket character.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/breakpoint_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's breakpoint icon color (displayed in the gutter).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/caret_background_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's caret background color. [b]Note:[/b] This setting has no effect as it's currently unused.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/caret_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's caret color.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/code_folding_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for the code folding icon (displayed in the gutter).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's comment color. [b]Note:[/b] In GDScript, unlike Python, multiline strings are not considered to be comments, and will use the string highlighting color instead.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/critical_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's critical comment marker text color. These markers are determined by [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/critical_list].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/critical_list
rawMembers.summary: A comma-separated list of case-sensitive words to highlight in comments. The text will be highlighted in the script editor with the [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/critical_color] color. These must not include spaces or symbols or they will not be highlighted. [b]Note:[/b] This is only implemented in the GDScript syntax highlighter.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/notice_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's notice comment marker text color. These markers are determined by [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/notice_list].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/notice_list
rawMembers.summary: A comma-separated list of case-sensitive words to highlight in comments. The text will be highlighted in the script editor with the [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/notice_color] color. These must not include spaces or symbols or they will not be highlighted. [b]Note:[/b] This is only implemented in the GDScript syntax highlighter.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/warning_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's warning comment marker text color. These markers are determined by [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/warning_list].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/warning_list
rawMembers.summary: A comma-separated list of case-sensitive words to highlight in comments. The text will be highlighted in the script editor with the [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/comment_markers/warning_color] color. These must not include spaces or symbols or they will not be highlighted. [b]Note:[/b] This is only implemented in the GDScript syntax highlighter.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/completion_background_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's autocompletion box background color.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/completion_existing_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's autocompletion box background color to highlight existing characters in the completion results. This should be a translucent color so that [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/completion_selected_color] can be seen behind.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/completion_font_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's autocompletion box text color.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/completion_scroll_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's autocompletion box scroll bar color.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/completion_scroll_hovered_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's autocompletion box scroll bar color when hovered or pressed with the mouse.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/completion_selected_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's autocompletion box background color for the currently selected line.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/control_flow_keyword_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's control flow keyword color (used for keywords like [code]if[/code], [code]for[/code], [code]return[/code], ...).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/current_line_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background color for the line the caret is currently on. This should be set to a translucent color so that it can display on top of other line color modifiers such as [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/mark_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/doc_comment_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's documentation comment color. In GDScript, this is used for comments starting with [code]##[/code]. In C#, this is used for comments starting with [code]///[/code] or [code]/**[/code].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/engine_type_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's engine type color ([Object], [Mesh], [Node], ...).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/executing_line_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for the debugger's executing line icon (displayed in the gutter).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/folded_code_region_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background line highlighting color for folded code region.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/function_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's function call color. [b]Note:[/b] When using the GDScript syntax highlighter, this is only used when calling some functions since function definitions and global functions have their own colors [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/function_definition_color] and [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/global_function_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/annotation_color
rawMembers.summary: The GDScript syntax highlighter text color for annotations (e.g. [code]@export[/code]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/function_definition_color
rawMembers.summary: The GDScript syntax highlighter text color for function definitions (e.g. the [code]_ready[/code] in [code]func _ready():[/code]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/global_function_color
rawMembers.summary: The GDScript syntax highlighter text color for global functions, such as the ones in [@GlobalScope] (e.g. [code]preload()[/code]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/node_path_color
rawMembers.summary: The GDScript syntax highlighter text color for [NodePath] literals (e.g. [code]^"position:x"[/code]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/node_reference_color
rawMembers.summary: The GDScript syntax highlighter text color for node reference literals (e.g. [code]$"Sprite"[/code] and [code]%"Sprite"[/code]]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/gdscript/string_name_color
rawMembers.summary: The GDScript syntax highlighter text color for [StringName] literals (e.g. [code]&"example"[/code]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/keyword_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's non-control flow keyword color (used for keywords like [code]var[/code], [code]func[/code], [code]extends[/code], ...).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/line_length_guideline_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for the line length guideline. The "hard" line length guideline will be drawn with this color, whereas the "soft" line length guideline will be drawn with half of its opacity.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/line_number_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for line numbers. See also [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/safe_line_number_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/mark_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background color for lines with errors. This should be set to a translucent color so that it can display on top of other line color modifiers such as [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/current_line_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/member_variable_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for member variables on objects (e.g. [code]self.some_property[/code]). [b]Note:[/b] This color is not used for local variable declaration and access.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/number_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for numbers (integer and floating-point).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/safe_line_number_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for type-safe line numbers. See also [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/line_number_color]. [b]Note:[/b] Only displayed if [member text_editor/appearance/gutters/highlight_type_safe_lines] is [code]true[/code].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/search_result_border_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for the border of search results. This border helps bring further attention to the search result. Set this color's opacity to 0 to disable the border.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/search_result_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background color for search results.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/selection_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background color for the currently selected text.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/string_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for strings (single-line and multi-line).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/string_placeholder_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for string placeholders, such as [code]%s[/code] and [code]{_}[/code]. Refer to the [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_format_string.html]GDScript format strings documentation[/url] for more details. [b]Note:[/b] Only the default [code]{_}[/code] placeholder patterns are highlighted for the [method String.format] method. Custom patterns still appear as plain strings.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/symbol_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for operators ([code]( ) [ ] { } + - * /[/code], ...).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/text_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for text not highlighted by any syntax highlighting rule.

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/text_selected_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background color for text. This should be set to a translucent color so that it can display on top of other line color modifiers such as [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/current_line_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/user_type_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for user-defined types (using [code]class_name[/code]).

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/warning_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's background color for lines with warnings. This should be set to a translucent color so that it can display on top of other line color modifiers such as [member text_editor/theme/highlighting/current_line_color].

rawMembers.name: text_editor/theme/highlighting/word_highlighted_color
rawMembers.summary: The script editor's color for words highlighted by selecting them. Only visible if [member text_editor/appearance/caret/highlight_all_occurrences] is [code]true[/code].

rawMembers.name: version_control/ssh_private_key_path
rawMembers.summary: Path to private SSH key file for the editor's Version Control integration credentials.

rawMembers.name: version_control/ssh_public_key_path
rawMembers.summary: Path to public SSH key file for the editor's Version Control integration credentials.

rawMembers.name: version_control/username
rawMembers.summary: Default username for editor's Version Control integration.

signals.name: SettingsChanged
signals.summary: Emitted after any editor setting has changed.

constants.name: NOTIFICATION_EDITOR_SETTINGS_CHANGED
constants.summary: Emitted after any editor setting has changed. It's used by various editor plugins to update their visuals on theme changes or logic on configuration changes.
